pattern: A common string divisor exists only when the two concatenate the same both ways; its length is the gcd of the two lengths.
hints: If any string divides both, then writing one after the other gives the same result regardless of order.
Check whether str1+str2 equals str2+str1; if not, no common divisor exists and the answer is empty.
When they agree, the answer is the prefix whose length is the greatest common divisor of the two string lengths.

edge_inputs.description: one is a whole repetition of the other
lengths share a gcd but contents disagree

stress.description: build a random base unit, repeat it different counts, sometimes corrupt one to break the divisor
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    ulen = rng.randint(1, min(20, cap))
    unit = ''.join(chr(65 + rng.randint(0, 3)) for _ in range(ulen))
    r1 = rng.randint(1, max(1, cap // ulen))
    r2 = rng.randint(1, max(1, cap // ulen))
    s1 = unit * r1
    s2 = unit * r2
    if rng.random() < 0.4 and len(s1) >= 1:
        i = rng.randrange(len(s1))
        s1 = s1[:i] + chr(65 + ((ord(s1[i]) - 65 + 1) % 4)) + s1[i+1:]
    return [s1[:1000], s2[:1000]]
stress.note: mix of true divisors and corrupted cases
